source_section.title_label_raw: General Land Use Requirements Access Uses Prohibited

provisions.heading_context_raw: Use Requirements Access Uses Prohibited
provisions.text: It is prohibited to access a use in one zone from a different zone, unless:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Use Requirements Access Uses Prohibited
provisions.text: the use being accessed is permitted in both zones; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Use Requirements Access Uses Prohibited
provisions.text: the access from one zone to a different zone is specifically permitted in Tables 1A, 1B, 1C, or 1D. Exterior Lighting

sections.title_label_raw: Exterior lighting shall not be directed towards abutting lots or streets.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Exterior lighting shall not be directed towards abutting lots or streets.
provisions.text: Exterior lighting shall not be directed towards abutting lots or streets. Recreational Vehicles

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding when used as a temporary construction use, a recreational

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding when used as a temporary construction use, a recreational
provisions.text: Excluding when used as a temporary construction use, a recreational vehicle shall not be used as a dwelling unit or a backyard suite use. Landmark Buildings

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark buildings, as shown on Schedule 9, may be internally converted to any use listed in the INS zone with a white circle containing the number 8 or the number 31 in black text (⑧)(㉛), (RCCC- Dec10/25;E Dec31/25) as shown in Table 1C, subject to the following requirements:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: the building existed on the coming into force date of this By-law;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: an expansion of the footprint of the building shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: not exceed 20%,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: not be located within a front or flanking yard, and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: be set back a minimum distance of 1.5 metres further from the streetline than the existing streetwall;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: subject to Section 103, the height of the existing building shall be maintained;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: dormer additions within the roof are permitted, but shall not exceed 50% of the building depth or building width;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: balconies are permitted, but shall meet the requirements of Section 96; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: commercial uses shall not exceed 25% of the building's floor area.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: No minimum number of motor vehicle parking spaces shall be required for any landmark building converted to a use permitted in Subsection 43(1).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 43(2) and 43(3), landmark
provisions.text: The amenity space requirements contained in Section 67 shall apply to any conversion resulting in a high-density dwelling use. Cannabis-Related Uses

sections.title_label_raw: Cannabis retail sales uses and cannabis lounge uses are only

provisions.heading_context_raw: Cannabis retail sales uses and cannabis lounge uses are only
provisions.text: Cannabis retail sales uses and cannabis lounge uses are only permitted if such facilities are operated by the Nova Scotia Liquor Corporation (NSLC).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Cannabis retail sales uses and cannabis lounge uses are only
provisions.text: Where a lot containing a cannabis production facility use abuts any lot containing a residential use, daycare use, community recreation use, school use, or religious institution use, the cannabis production facility use, including any building or storage yard, shall be set back no less than 70.0 metres from the abutting lot line. Temporary Construction Uses Permitted

sections.title_label_raw: A development permit is required for a temporary construction use.

provisions.heading_context_raw: A development permit is required for a temporary construction use.
provisions.text: A development permit is required for a temporary construction use. Temporary Rock Crushers

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required for a temporary rock crusher and shall be valid for the time specified on such permit and which shall not exceed 60 calendar days from the time the permit is issued. The development permit for a temporary rock crusher may be renewed for a period of not longer than 30 calendar days at a time, if the Development Officer determines that an extension is warranted.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: A development permit for the use of a temporary rock crusher accessory to the construction of primary or secondary services, pursuant to the HRM Regional Subdivision By-Law, shall be valid for any period not exceeding the construction time schedule specified in the subdivision agreement.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: A temporary rock crusher shall only be used at:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: the site of demolition of a structure;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: the site of construction of primary or secondary services, pursuant to the HRM Regional Subdivision By-Law; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: the site of a development permitted by this By-law.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: A temporary rock crusher shall not be located within 3.0 metres of any lot line.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: A temporary rock crusher shall not be located within 10.0 metres of any building used for a residential use or an institutional use, except for public infrastructure.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 46(7), a temporary rock crusher shall not be used to process material for export to another site, or to process material imported to the site.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 46(2), a development permit is required
provisions.text: A temporary rock crusher may be used to process demolished material for export to a disposal site, if the requirements of HRM By-law L-200, the C&D Materials Recycling and Disposal License By-law, as amended from time to time, are met. Uses Near Railways

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 47(2), there shall be a minimum required

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 47(2), there shall be a minimum required
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 47(2), there shall be a minimum required separation distance of 15.0 metres between the centreline of any railway track and:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 47(2), there shall be a minimum required
provisions.text: any new high-density dwelling use; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 47(2), there shall be a minimum required
provisions.text: excluding indoor parking areas and storage areas, any new main building located in a CDD-2 or CDD-1 zone.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 47(2), there shall be a minimum required
provisions.text: If the separation distance required in Subsection 47(1) cannot be provided, a report prepared and stamped by a professional engineer shall be submitted to the Development Officer, identifying measures that will be used to mitigate the crash risk, noise, and vibration of trains, before a development permit may be issued.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 47(2), there shall be a minimum required
provisions.text: A development permit issued in accordance with Subsection 47(2) shall require the development to include the mitigation measures identified in the report submitted under Subsection 47(2).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 47(2), there shall be a minimum required
provisions.text: The mitigation measures, identified in the report submitted under Subsection 47(2), shall be completed and certified by a professional engineer prior to the issuance of the first occupancy permit. Solid Waste Management Areas

sections.title_label_raw: For any building in a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2,

provisions.heading_context_raw: For any building in a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2,
provisions.text: For any building in a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2, HR-1, CLI, INS, UC-2, or UC-1 zone that is greater than 2,000 square metres in floor area, all solid waste management areas shall be located inside the building.

provisions.heading_context_raw: For any building in a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2,
provisions.text: For a multi-unit dwelling use, any outdoor solid waste management areas shall be located in a side or rear yard, and shall not be located within 3.5 metres of any lot line abutting a residential use (Diagram 23). (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: For any building in a DD, DH, CEN-2, CEN-1, COR, HR-2,
provisions.text: Screening requirements for outdoor solid waste management areas are contained in Section 424.
